hypothesis: If, for direct-force pre-trained neural network interatomic potentials, the root-mean-square difference between conservative forces (−∇ĤE) and directly predicted non-conservative forces (Force Delta, UΔ) is used as an uncertainty metric, then UΔ will strongly correlate with the true force error (RMS difference to DFT forces) on equilibrium and adversarial out-of-distribution structures and will detect failures more reliably than within-family ensemble variance as a data-free UQ measure.
